original_image_tags: Scan of a rectangular, off-white envelope. The envelope is addressed to J. Edgar Hoover. A circular postmark and a rectangular slogan cancel are visible. A purple 3-cent U.S. postage stamp featuring the head of George Washington is affixed to the upper right corner. A small, dark hole (likely a staple or fastener hole) is present on the right side.

A purple 3-cent United States Postage stamp. It features a profile portrait of George Washington facing left. The text at the top reads "UNITED STATES POSTAGE". The denomination "3 CENTS 3" is at the bottom. A small, partially obscured text is visible at the bottom right, likely "LIBERTY ISSUE" or similar.

elements.description: A United States 3-cent purple postage stamp featuring a profile portrait and the text 'UNITED STATES POSTAGE' and '3 CENTS 3'. A circular postmark reads 'SAN FRANCISCO CALIF. / JUL 21 / 1-PM / 1947'. A slogan cancellation reads 'BUILD YOUR [faint] WISELY, SAV[e] / U.S. SAVINGS BONDS'.

judge.reason: Both agree on the stamp and postmark content, but Description B adds specific verbatim text for the postmark and slogan cancel that Description A only describes generically.
